reasoning: EXPE has experienced a massive 37% run-up since early November 2025, reaching $288.6 — well above the average analyst price target of ~$232, and virtually at the latest upgraded peak target of $290 (RBC). This strongly suggests upside resistance and an elevated likelihood of short-term profit-taking or mean reversion. The yfinance tool gives a 0.41 probability of the price being higher over the 1-week horizon, and historical data from the same period last year saw a price drop between late December and January. Further, a director recently sold shares, adding to signs of a local top. For the 1-week horizon, I estimate a 44% chance of resolving higher. Over the 1-month and 3-month horizons (which crosses the Q4 earnings report in early February), uncertainty increases and positive market drift becomes a stronger factor. Accordingly, my probabilities taper back towards 0.50.
